title: Cyclophorus lauterbachii
taxonomy: Cyclophorus lauterbachii is classified under the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Cyclophoridae. This family belongs to the phylum Mollusca, but in a broader sense, it is also referred to as a genus of land snails. More specifically, Cyclophorus lauterbachii is a species of air-breathing snail, falling under the class Gastropoda. As a member of the Gastropoda class, it is further divided into the subclass Pulmonata, which is characterized by air-breathing snails and slugs. Within the Pulmonata subclass, Cyclophorus lauterbachii can be categorized as belonging to the infraclass Eupulmonata.
benefits: Cyclophorus lauterbachii is a species of plant that has been researched for its potential health benefits, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. Some studies suggest that the plant may have antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which could aid in the prevention and treatment of certain diseases. However, more research is needed to fully understand the health benefits of Cyclophorus lauterbachii. Further studies may provide more insight into the plant's potential uses and applications.

intro: Cyclophorus lauterbachii, also known as Lauterbach's Cyclophorus, is a species of plant in the family Cyclophoridae, which is a type of land snail shell-bearing freshwater snail. Traditionally, Cyclophorus lauterbachii has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cyclophorus lauterbachii include teas, infusions, and extracts, often used to treat fever and relieve symptoms of colds and flu.
